scores.justification: The related-work section engages the prior art the area's experts would expect for v4's claims (form compilers, libCEED foil, differentiable/GPU EM, qubit-EM workflows, V&V tradition) on the merits, not as a citation list; 50 complete entries, zero unused. (The v5 reframe adds a differentiable-design axis requiring a litsearch re-run — see comments.md.)

scores.fix: Resolve the TODO(operator) repository URL and archival DOI so the committed artifacts are reachable by an arXiv reader.
scores.justification: Methods and per-cell provenance are detailed enough to replicate, and every number traces to a committed artifact — but the repository URL/DOI footnote is still a TODO(operator), so no stable public artifact reference exists yet from the preprint alone.

Contributions are stated one sentence per item in the introduction and are extractable from the abstract; a triaging reader gets the claim, the evidence standard, and the honest negatives from the first page.

physics.comp-ph (cross-list quant-ph) is correct for a solver/method/cross-validation paper as v4 stands; the abstract's framing matches comp-ph reader expectations. Under the v5 differentiable-design headline the quant-ph cross-list grows stronger; category stays valid.

findings.rationale: arXiv readers triage reproducibility on a working repository link; the footnote defers it to submission time.
findings.suggested_fix: Operator supplies the repository URL and archival DOI before submission.
